highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.110 Released
highlights.summary: The latest release adds a `/tui` command for flicker-free fullscreen rendering, making terminal output much smoother. The `Ctrl+O` shortcut now toggles verbose transcript mode, while a new `/focus` command handles focus view separately. This is a quality-of-life update worth installing right away if you use Claude Code heavily in the terminal.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.108 Released
highlights.summary: This release introduces the `ENABLE_PROMPT_CACHING_1H` environment variable, letting you opt into a 1-hour prompt cache TTL across API key, Bedrock, Vertex, and Foundry backends — which can significantly reduce costs on repeated large prompts. A new recap feature also provides context summaries to help Claude stay on track during long sessions. If you're running Claude Code in CI or with a backend provider, this update is particularly valuable.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.105 Released
highlights.summary: This release adds a `path` parameter to the `EnterWorktree` tool so you can switch into existing Git worktrees mid-session — great for multi-branch workflows. A new `PreCompact` hook lets your scripts block context compaction by exiting with code 2, giving you more control over when Claude compresses long conversations. Background monitor support for plugins was also added, expanding the automation possibilities.

highlights.title: Claude Code OAuth Down for Over 12 Hours
highlights.summary: Users on v2.1.107 reported being unable to complete OAuth login — the paste step in the browser auth flow was broken for many, leaving them stuck at the `Paste code here if prompted` screen. If you hit this issue, upgrading to v2.1.108 or later is the recommended fix, as the problem appears resolved in newer releases. This is a good reminder to keep Claude Code updated and to check the status page if auth suddenly stops working.

highlights.title: Claude Code Routines (Official Docs)
highlights.summary: Anthropic's official documentation on Claude Code Routines explains how to define reusable, automated task sequences that Claude can execute on command. Routines let you encode multi-step workflows — like running tests, committing, and deploying — into a single callable unit, saving repetitive prompting. This is a must-read for anyone looking to scale their Claude Code productivity beyond one-off tasks.

highlights.title: The Ultimate Claude Code Guide | MCP, Skills & More
highlights.summary: This YouTube video offers a comprehensive walkthrough of Claude Code's most powerful features, including how to set up and use MCP (Model Context Protocol) and define reusable skills. It's a strong intermediate-level resource for developers who have the basics down and want to unlock more advanced capabilities. Watch it to learn how MCP integrations can dramatically extend what Claude Code can do inside your projects.

highlights.title: FULL Claude Code Tutorial for Beginners in 2026! (Step-By-Step)
highlights.summary: A beginner-friendly step-by-step video tutorial covering everything you need to get started with Claude Code in 2026, from installation to your first AI-assisted coding session. This is one of the most up-to-date entry points for developers who are brand new to the tool. If you've been putting off learning Claude Code, this is the video to start with.

highlights.title: How to Use AI Programming in VS Code (Chinese Tutorial)
highlights.summary: This Bilibili video walks through how to set up AI coding assistance inside VS Code, covering extension installation, account registration, model selection, and generating and refining code. While it focuses on Chinese-market AI tools, the workflow concepts — install, authenticate, prompt, iterate — apply directly to Claude Code users in VS Code too. A helpful reference for Chinese-speaking developers getting started with AI-assisted coding.

tips_of_the_day: Run `/tui fullscreen` at the start of any long Claude Code session to enable flicker-free rendering — your eyes will thank you during extended coding sprints.

Set the `ENABLE_PROMPT_CACHING_1H` environment variable in your shell profile if you frequently work with large codebases or long CLAUDE.md files; the 1-hour cache TTL can meaningfully cut your API costs over a day of work.

If you define a Claude Code Routine for your most common multi-step tasks (like lint → test → commit), you can invoke the entire sequence with a single prompt instead of guiding Claude step by step every time.
